headline: Broadcom’s Google And Anthropic AI Deals Deepen Custom Chip Reliance
summary: Broadcom (NasdaqGS:AVGO) has signed multi-year AI chip and infrastructure agreements with Google and Anthropic. The deals run through 2031 and cover exclusive custom silicon design and supply for next generation AI platforms. The arrangements include AI accelerators and networking hardware that support large scale AI workloads. For investors watching AI infrastructure, this positions Broadcom, a major provider of custom chips and networking gear, closer to the core of how large models are...

headline: Nvidia and Its Big Tech Peers Are on a Bad Run. Goldman Sachs Says It’s Time to Buy.
summary: Big Tech stocks have taken a battering lately—but investors should take the opportunity to buy the dip as the Iran war drags on, according to Goldman Sachs.  Tech in 2026 has posted one of its worst periods of relative underperformance of the past 50 years amid a pivot to value stocks, analyst Peter Oppenheimer said in a research note on Tuesday.  Facebook owner  Meta Platforms  IT company  Microsoft  and chip maker  Nvidia  are all fetching less than 20 times expected earnings for the next 24 months, the analyst noted.

headline: Nvidia Stock Drops as Iran War Heats Up
summary: Nvidia  stock was falling in premarket trading Tuesday amid concerns that the Iran war was set to escalate.  Shares of Nvidia were down 1.4% early Monday, while  futures had fallen 0.4%, and  futures had declined 0.3%.  Nvidia stock took a leg lower with the stock market after reports that the U.S. had attacked military targets on Kharg Island, from which much of Iran’s oil is exported.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Apple, UnitedHealth, Broadcom, Arm, Delta, Intel, Arista, and More
summary: Stocks were sliding Tuesday after President Donald Trump declared that “a whole civilization will die tonight” ahead of his 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Apple  tumbled 3.4% after Nikkei Asia reported that the iPhone maker’s long-awaited foldable phone had encountered engineering challenges and could face shipping delays.  The federal government on Monday pledged to pay insurers in the Medicare program an average increase of 2.48% next year, well above an originally proposed rate of just 0.09% in January.

content.title: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Apple, UnitedHealth, Broadcom, Arm, Delta, Intel, Arista, and More
content.summary: Stocks were sliding Tuesday after President Donald Trump declared that “a whole civilization will die tonight” ahead of his 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Apple  tumbled 3.4% after Nikkei Asia reported that the iPhone maker’s long-awaited foldable phone had encountered engineering challenges and could face shipping delays.  The federal government on Monday pledged to pay insurers in the Medicare program an average increase of 2.48% next year, well above an originally proposed rate of just 0.09% in January.

headline: Are AI giants coming for bitcoin miners' power?
summary: Anthropic's multi-gigawatt compute deal with Google and Broadcom highlights how AI companies are now directly competing with bitcoin miners for the same energy infrastructure. Several major miners have already started converting capacity to AI hosting, but does this mean that bitcoin mining is dying? CoinDesk's Jennifer Sanasie hosts "CoinDesk Daily."

content.title: Are AI giants coming for bitcoin miners' power?
content.description: Anthropic's multi-gigawatt compute deal with Google and Broadcom highlights how AI companies are now directly competing with bitcoin miners for the same energy infrastructure. Several major miners have already started converting capacity to AI hosting, but does this mean that bitcoin mining is dying? CoinDesk's Jennifer Sanasie hosts "CoinDesk Daily."
content.summary: Anthropic's multi-gigawatt compute deal with Google and Broadcom highlights how AI companies are now directly competing with bitcoin miners for the same energy infrastructure. Several major miners have already started converting capacity to AI hosting, but does this mean that bitcoin mining is dying? CoinDesk's Jennifer Sanasie hosts "CoinDesk Daily."

headline: The Spiraling Cost of Making AI
summary: Spending money to make money is taking on a whole new meaning in the artificial-intelligence world.  The public is already familiar with the exorbitant capital spending of the world’s largest tech companies as they race to build up their AI capabilities.  OpenAI and Anthropic expect to spend nearly $65 billion combined this year just on the costs to train and operate their AI models, according to financial documents obtained by The Wall Street Journal.

content.title: The Spiraling Cost of Making AI
content.summary: Spending money to make money is taking on a whole new meaning in the artificial-intelligence world.  The public is already familiar with the exorbitant capital spending of the world’s largest tech companies as they race to build up their AI capabilities.  OpenAI and Anthropic expect to spend nearly $65 billion combined this year just on the costs to train and operate their AI models, according to financial documents obtained by The Wall Street Journal.

headline: Anthropic Tops $30 Billion Run Rate, Seals Broadcom Deal
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Anthropic PBC said its revenue run rate has now topped $30 billion, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025, and confirmed plans to work with Broadcom Inc. and Google to power its burgeoning operations.The AI startup said that demand for its Claude services has accelerated this year, with more than 1,000 business customers spending over $1 million on an annual basis. headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Improves As Hormuz Deadline Nears; Financial Name Breaks Out (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes pared losses Tuesday ahead of President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz.  Meanwhile, two names found strength and broke out of bases as UnitedHealth Group topped the Dow on the stock market today.  Coming off session lows, the Dow was down 0.3% while the S&P 500 also slipped 0.3%.

headline: Stock Market Today: Dow, S&P 500 Close Mixed As Critical Iran Deadline Nears (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes closed the session modestly mixed Tuesday with President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz quickly approaching.  In stock news, UnitedHealth Group bucked the trend with a hefty gain and was the biggest Dow winner on the stock market today.  Stocks jockeyed around in the final hour of trading with Dow ending 0.2% lower while the S&P 500 reversed and ended 0.1% higher.

headline: Where Nvidia Stock Needs to Trade to Get Out of Its Sideways Trap
summary: Nvidia  stock was down for much of Tuesday but ended the trading session up slightly amid concerns that the Iran war was set to escalate.  It’s still stuck in the purgatory of a nine-month trading range.  Nvidia stock rose 0.3% to $178.10, finishing well off an intraday low of $173.66; the  gained 0.1%, and  moved 0.2% lower.
